task_completion.description: Create the Top Event labeled 'Total Loss of Power'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rectangle or standard top event symbol at the top of the diagram explicitly labeled 'Total Loss of Power'.

task_completion.description: Model the Power Distribution Board (PDB) as a single point of failure
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PDB failure event should be connected directly as an input to a top-level OR gate that leads to the 'Total Loss of Power' event.

task_completion.description: Model battery redundancy using an AND gate
task_completion.visual_evidence: An AND gate symbol (flat bottom, rounded top) must be used to combine the failure paths of Battery A and Battery B, showing that BOTH must fail to cause a system power loss.

task_completion.description: Include at least 4 basic events for battery failure modes
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least 4 basic event symbols (circles or ovals) at the bottom of the tree representing root causes (e.g., 'Internal Cell Malfunction', 'Connector Disconnect' for each battery).

task_completion.description: Save as drawio and export as PNG to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: Icons for 'fault_tree.drawio' and 'fault_tree.png' visible on the Desktop, or file manager/save dialogs confirming both files are saved to the ~/Desktop directory.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical diagramming tool to create the fault tree
integrity.visual_evidence: A diagramming application (like draw.io/diagrams.net) is open and visible in the screenshots, rather than a terminal script or text editor generating the files.

integrity.description: The agent actively built the diagram on the canvas
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show intermediate steps of shapes being dragged, selected, labeled, or connected with lines on the canvas.

integrity.description: The exported PNG matches the constructed diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: The final diagram visible in the editor canvas matches the content of the exported 'fault_tree.png' file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The fault tree must accurately reflect the system logic: 1) It requires exactly 1 AND gate to represent the battery redundancy (Battery A AND Battery B must fail). 2) It requires at least 2 OR gates (e.g., the top-level gate where PDB failure OR Battery System failure leads to Total Loss of Power). 3) It requires at least 4 basic events (circles) representing the two specific failure modes ('Internal Cell Malfunction' and 'Connector Disconnect') for each of the two batteries.
